revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Azuma Kofun]][[ja:吾妻古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Azuma Kofun | native_name =吾妻古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Azuma-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption =Azuma Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Azuma Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.404211|N|139.811425|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|qid=Q1354910|lt=Mibu|1=Mibu, Tochigi}} / [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi]], {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Azuma Kofun'''|吾妻古墳 ||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located on the border of the town of {{ill|Mibu, Tochigi|en|Mibu, Tochigi|qid=Q1354910|lt=Mibu|1=Mibu, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|en|Shimotsuga District, Tochigi|qid=Q1202834|lt=Shimotsuga District|1=Shimotsuga District, Tochigi}}, and the city of [[:en:Tochigi, Tochigi|Tochigi]] in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1970.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/487 |title=吾妻古墳|trans-title= Azuma kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Azuma Kofun is located on a narrow plateau between the Ogawa and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|qid=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}. As a result of an [[archaeological excavation]] from 2007 to 2010, it was determined that the tumulus is a {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} built in two tiers. The lower tier (base) is wide, and with a total length of about 128 meters was found to be the largest in the prefecture. The second tier is about 86 meters long with a height of about 10 meters. The two-tiered mound is set on a base of similar shape, and is surrounded by a moat with a width of about 20 meters and a depth of about three meters. {{ill|Pumice|en|Pumice|qid=Q180044|lt=Pumice|1=Pumice}} deposits from the 1108 AD eruption of {{ill|Mount Asama|en|Mount Asama|qid=Q242730|lt=Mount Asama|1=Mount Asama}} were confirmed from the soil filling the moat. The [[:en:Burial chamber|burial facility]] is a horizontal hole type stone chamber with dimensions of 2.4 meters long x 1.7 meters wide x 2.0 meters high; however, during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the front part of the rectangular portion of the tumulus was destroyed, and the entrance stone and one of the ceiling stones were removed by the ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' of {{ill|Mibu Domain|en|Mibu Domain|qid=Q3034496|lt=Mibu Domain|1=Mibu Domain}} for use as ornamental stones in the gardens of {{ill|Mibu Castle|en|Mibu Castle|qid=Q11429498|lt=Mibu Castle|1=Mibu Castle}}, where they can still be seen. The entrance stone was of cut {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} with a rectangular opening for a door, whereas the ceiling stone (and remaining stones in the walls and remaining ceiling of the burial chamber) are of monolithic {{ill|diorite|en|diorite|qid=Q187408|lt=diorite|1=Diorite}}. Red pigment was applied to the walls of the burial chamber, which was backfilled for preservation after the 2010 excavation.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> The surface of the tumulus is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. Fragments of cylindrical and house-shaped ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. The tumulus is a representative example of a ''kofun'' of the late Kofun period, from around the latter half of the 6th century. Recovered artifacts, including small objects of gold and copper found in the burial chamber, are on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-ga-oka Museum at site, which is located approximately eight minutes by car from {{ill|Mibu Station|en|Mibu Station|qid=Q6827788|lt=Mibu Station|1=Mibu Station}} on the [[:en:Tōbu Railway|Tōbu Railway]] {{ill|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|en|Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|qid=Q3449834|lt=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line|1=Tōbu Utsunomiya Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==Gallery== <gallery> Azuma Kofun genmon.JPG|Entrance monolith to Azuma Kofun now relocated to Mibu Castle Azuma Kofun tenjou.JPG|Ceiling monolith to Azuma Kofun now relocated to Mibu Castle </gallery> ==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan|qq=}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]] ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== [[Category:broken commons categories]] * [https://www.tochigiji.or.jp/spot/5060/ Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} * [http://www.mibu.ed.jp/d_map/link/0005/P0005.html Mibu Department of Education] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
